summary: Audience recording of an obscure 1971 show captured by a nearby taper, yielding decent sound despite limited performance details.
review: Both reviewers are impressed that at least one dedicated taper was present and positioned close to the stage, which they credit for providing usable audio. Aside from historical interest, no musical or technical criticisms are offered, leaving the actual performance quality unaddressed.

ai_rating.rationale: Both reviewers gave five‑star ratings based on taper presence and rarity; however, no concrete comments on sound fidelity or musical performance are provided, so the rating reflects a positive but uncertain assessment.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers note that they were standing near the taper and received better sound than typical distant audience recordings; no complaints about muddiness or dropouts are mentioned.
